'''Adam Keighran''' (born 24 April 1997) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Betfred Super League]].

He previously played for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] and the [[Sydney Roosters]] in the [[National Rugby League]]. Keighran also played for the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]]. Earlier in his career he played as a {{rlp|so}} and {{rlp|hk}}.

==Background==
Keighran was born in Sydney, Australia.

He played his junior rugby league for the [[Wyong Roos]] before being signed by the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]].

==Career==
===Penrith Panthers reserves===
[[File:Adam Keighran.jpg|thumb|right|Keighran playing for the Penrith Panthers in 2018]]
Keighran signed a two-year deal in 2017 with the [[Penrith Panthers]], kicking 80 goals for their [[New South Wales Cup]] side in 2018 and was picked as one of two centres of the year despite playing the last 10 games of his season in the halves.{{citation needed|date=April 2024}}

===New Zealand Warriors===
[[File:Adam Keighran Warriors.jpg|thumb|right|Keighran warming up for the New Zealand Warriors in 2019]]
In October 2018 he signed a three-year deal with the [[New Zealand Warriors]].

Keighran made his first grade debut in round 1 of the [[2019 NRL season]] playing for the Warriors against the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]], scoring a try in their 40–6 win.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/03/12/round-1-nrl-team-lists/ |title=Round 1 NRL team lists|publisher=NRL.com|date=12 March 2019}}</ref>

===Sydney Roosters===
In November 2020, he signed a two-year deal to join the [[Sydney Roosters]] starting in 2021.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.roosters.com.au/news/2020/11/29/adam-keighran-joins-the-roosters/|title=Adam Keighran joins the Roosters|website=www.roosters.com.au|date=28 November 2020 }}</ref>

In round 1 of the [[2021 NRL season]], he made his debut for the [[Sydney Roosters]] in their 46–4 victory over Manly-Warringah at the [[Sydney Cricket Ground]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-03-13/nrl-sydney-roosters-penrith-score-big-first-round-wins/13243368/|title=Sydney Roosters break first-round record with 46-4 win over Manly, Penrith Panthers thump Cowboys 46-4|newspaper=ABC News|date=13 March 2021 }}</ref>
In round 25 of the [[2021 NRL season]], he scored a [[hat-trick]] for the [[Sydney Roosters]] in a 40–16 victory over [[Canberra Raiders|Canberra]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2021-canberra-raiders-vs-sydney-roosters-live-blog-live-stream-supercoach-scores-videos-jack-wighton-james-tedesco/news-story/745b280dcc746da257fecd8fda231afd/|title=Roosters' 'Mr Fix It' stuns with hat-trick, Sloppy Raiders' final fade out ends season: 3 Big Hits|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=2 September 2021 }}</ref>

He played a total of 15 games for the [[Sydney Roosters]] in the [[2021 NRL season]] including the club's two finals matches.  The [[Sydney Roosters]] would be eliminated from the second week of the finals losing to Manly 42–6.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/09/16/they-dont-rate-us-how-injury-toll-eased-pressure-for-revitalised-roosters/|title='They don't rate us': How injury toll eased pressure for revitalised Roosters|website=www.nrl.com|date=16 September 2021}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-09-17/nrl-manly-sea-eagles-defeat-sydney-roosters-in-semi-final/100472900/|title=Manly Sea Eagles hammer Sydney Roosters 42-6 to advance to NRL preliminary finals|newspaper=ABC News|date=17 September 2021}}</ref>

Keighran only featured in two matches for the Sydney Roosters throughout the [[2022 NRL season]]. Keighran spent the rest of the year playing for the club's [[NSW Cup]] feeder team [[North Sydney Bears|North Sydney]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-sydney-roosters-season-review-ins-and-outs-team-news-trent-robinson-matt-lodge-joseph-sualii/news-story/382f8d30c3ace5024a0c4f25d1920153/|title=Robbo switch that woke sleeping giant as full extent of cap crunch laid bare: Brutal Review|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=12 September 2022 }}</ref>

===Catalans Dragons===
On 23 November 2022, Keighran signed a contract to join [[Super League]] side the [[Catalans Dragons]] ahead of the 2023 Super League season. Keighran made his club debut for Catalans in their round 1 victory over [[Wakefield Trinity]] kicking five goals.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/catalans-dragons-adam-keighran-roosters-25590298/|title=Catalans Dragons complete deal for Sydney Roosters centre|website=www.examinerlive.co.uk}}</ref>

In round 12, Keighran scored two tries and kicked five goals for Catalans as they defeated [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] 46–22.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65545804|title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 22-46 Catalans Dragons - Tigers suffer fourth straight loss|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

On 16 July, Keighran signed a two-year deal to join Wigan starting in 2024.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://wiganwarriors.com/blog/2023/07/16/keighran-signs-for-wigan/|title=Keighran signs for Wigan|website=wiganwarriors.com}}</ref>
In round 20, Keighran scored two tries and kicked four goals as Catalans defeated Salford 42–0.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12930272/catalans-dragons-42-0-salford-red-devils-dragons-hammer-red-devils-to-remain-top-of-super-league/|title=Catalans Dragons 42-0 Salford Red Devils: Dragons hammer Red Devils to remain top of Super League|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

On 14 October 2023, Keighran played in Catalans [[2023 Super League Grand Final]] loss against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]. Keighran became the second player in Super League history to be sin binned during a grand final after he was sent from the field for a dangerous lifting tackle in the first half.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/oct/14/catalans-dragons-wigan-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Wigan Warriors crowned Super League champions after downing Dragons|newspaper=The Observer|date=14 October 2023 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref>

===Wigan Warriors===
In round 1 of the 2024 Super League season, Keighran made his club debut for Wigan in their 32–4 victory over Castleford.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68304778|title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 4-32 Wigan Warriors - Reigning champions begin with hard-earned win|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

On 24 February, Keighran played in Wigan's [[2024 World Club Challenge]] final victory over [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68337685|title=World Club Challenge: Wigan Warriors 16-12 Penrith Panthers - Cherry and Whites crowned world champions for fifth time|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
In round 4, Keighran scored one try and kicked ten goals in Wigan's 60–22 victory over the newly promoted [[London Broncos|London]] team.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68501497|title=Super League: London Broncos 22-60 Wigan Warriors - reigning champions go top again|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
In round 13, Keighran was given a straight red card for a dangerous high tackle during Wigan's 19–18 victory over [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] and subsequently missed the [[Challenge Cup]] final.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cp33kydn2veo|title=Wigan hit back to edge youthful Warrington|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 12 October, Keighran played in Wigan's 9-2 2024 Super League grand final victory over Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2024/oct/12/wigan-v-hull-kr-super-league-grand-final-2024-live-score-updates?page=with:block-670aaacb8f087ed1d5a8a350/|title=Wigan v Hull KR: Super League Grand Final 2024 – as it happened|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

In 2025, Keighran suffered a [[medial collateral ligament]] injury, ruling him out for four months.<ref>https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wigan-warriors-coach-confirms-huge-injury-blow-as-jai-field-update-provided</ref> In July, he signed a new three-year deal with Wigan.<ref>[https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c0j458gv40po bbc.co.uk]</ref>
On 9 October 2025, Keighran played in Wigan's 24-6 2025 Super League Grand Final loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2jw497jpzo/|title=Hull KR beat Wigan in Grand Final to complete treble|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 30 May 2026, Keighran played in Wigan's 2026 Challenge Cup final victory against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2026/jun/01/challenge-cup-crowds-are-dwindling-but-rugby-league-must-save-wembley-relationship/|title=Challenge Cup crowds are dwindling but rugby league must save Wembley relationship|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>
